name: Centre-Val de Loire (France)

colors.symbolism: the field surrounding the gonfalon, and the label of three points
the gonfalon-style ornamental bands evoking the historic Orleanais gonfalon
the azure field of the historic royal arms of France
the gold (or) of the three fleurs-de-lis

description: A white field with red gonfalon-style ornamental bands, with a central blue panel charged with three gold fleurs-de-lis surmounted by a white label of three points (the historic arms of the House of Orleans).
history: Centre-Val de Loire is a French region whose territory corresponds broadly to the historic Duchy of Orleans. France traditionally does not promote regional flags; this banner of the historic Orleans arms is used in a de-facto capacity rather than as an officially adopted flag of the modern administrative region.

features.symbolism: A white field bearing the gonfalon-style banner of the historic Duchy of Orleans, with a central blue panel charged with three gold fleurs-de-lis and a white label of three points (the cadency mark of the House of Orleans).

features.role: the three fleurs-de-lis of France modern, on an azure field
features.symbolism: The three gold fleurs-de-lis on azure are the arms of the kings of France, here charged with a label as the cadency mark of the House of Orleans, the historic dukes whose lands form the heart of the modern region.
The label of three points is the heraldic cadency mark identifying the cadet branch of the House of Orleans.
